---
title: "Pipeline"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

The measurement is a [Kedro](https://kedro.org) project: pure algorithms in `transfer_risk.lib`, thin nodes that read and write through a typed Data Catalog, and pipelines wired one stage at a time (data, models, similarity, attacks, transfer, risk, reporting). The graph below is the live Kedro-Viz export — pan, zoom, and click a node or dataset to see its inputs, outputs, and layer.

This is the full production graph: ten surrogates and five attack recipes (the 50 `(surrogate, recipe)` cells), with the attack stage splitting each cell into a few example-shards so a slow cell still spreads across cores. The shard count scales to the run box — about `2 × vCPUs` shards in total (~3 per cell here) — rather than a fixed tiny size that would fan out to thousands of nodes. `ParallelRunner` runs the shards across cores and `kedro run --only-missing-outputs` resumes. Click a node or dataset to see its inputs, outputs, and layer; use the focus and pipeline filters to isolate a stage.

```{=html}
<iframe class="viz-frame" src="pipeline-viz/index.html" title="Kedro pipeline graph"></iframe>
```
